top_signals.title: UAE's exit from OPEC+ reduced the group's share of crude oil production and capacity
top_signals.summary: On April 28, 2026, the United Arab Emirates (UAE) announced that it was leaving OPEC, effective on May 1. OPEC was formed in 1960 by Iraq, Iran, Kuwait, Saudi Arabia, and Venezuela, with the stated objective to "coordinate and unify petroleum policies among Member Countries." OPEC is best known for its effect on global crude oil prices.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Crude Oil Inventories Fall Less Than Expected, Signaling Lower Demand
top_signals.summary: The American Petroleum Institute (API) has released its latest data on U.S. crude oil inventories, revealing a smaller-than-anticipated decline. The API Weekly Crude Stock report showed a decrease of 0.399 million barrels in crude oil inventories. This figure falls short of market expectations, which had forecasted a more substantial decline of 1.500 million barrels. The ...

top_signals.title: Has Iraq Finally Run Out of Road in Its Double-Dealings with the West?
top_signals.summary: Late June/early July normally brings twin events from Iraq for the global oil industry: an announcement that it intends to increase its crude oil production to either 6 or 7 million barrels per day (bpd) within three years, and a statement that its prime minister will visit Washington to discuss deepening strategic ties or something similar aimed at securing money from the U.S. This year is no different, with the announced three-year target being 7 million bpd, and the visit of new prime minister, Ali al-Zaidi happening in the middle of this month.…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.
